DPDP Act, 2023 · Product Management Case Study

Unified Privacy & Consent Hub
for YouTube

One privacy architecture wearing three age-appropriate faces — Adults, Teens (13–17) and Children under 13 — designed end to end against India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.

PP Prepared by Prathiba P · Product Management Intern Case Study · July 2026
Project Overview

One hub. Three journeys. One backend.

YouTube today treats "under 13" and "everyone else" as the only two privacy states that exist. DPDP draws the line at 18 — leaving over 40M Indian teens on a fully personalised adult product with no verifiable parental consent on record. This case study designs the fix: a single Privacy & Consent Hub that adapts by age rather than forking into separate products.

● Adult · 18+

Arjun, 27

Self-consenting Data Principal. Explicit, purpose-based, revocable consent — control without friction, not a lockdown.

Walk the journey →
● Teen · 13–17

Priya, 15 & Meera, 42

The flagship deep-dive: verified-parent link via DigiLocker/OTP, purpose-based consent, and a teen-facing dashboard.

Walk the journey →
● Child · Under 13

Aarav, 9

Parent-managed Data Principal on YouTube Kids. No behavioural tracking, no targeted ads, no self-configuration expected.

Walk the journey →
Explore the Case Study

Everything a reviewer needs, in one place

Architecture Summary

One backend, age-aware at every layer

An API Gateway routes every request through Age Routing Logic before it reaches the Privacy, Consent, or Identity Verification services — so personalisation, ads, and notifications are shaped by age before any downstream service ever sees the request.
